title: UUPS upgrade authorization is completely unrestricted in `Proxiable` / `ChildOfProxiable`
reason: The code is confined to `contracts/test/Proxiable.sol`, and no production contract in the provided source references or inherits it; this is test-only scaffolding, not a deployed-system issue from the evidence available.

title: Owner Can Drain All Pool Funds via Token Status Manipulation
reason: This depends on owner-only privilege changes and existing pool debt; `rebalancePool` only transfers up to the accounted `vcashDebt`/collateralized amount and appears to be an intended privileged recovery path, not a permissionless drain primitive from source alone.

title: Missing Zero Address Check for feeTo
reason: This is an owner misconfiguration issue rather than an exploit path; it does not let an attacker steal or lock user funds permissionlessly.

title: Excessive Fee Setting Allowed
reason: Bounded fee changes by the owner are governance/centralization risk, not a code vulnerability.

title: Direct Swap Logic Always Returns False
reason: The condition is unusual but not always false: due to integer division it returns true when pool values are within roughly a 2x band, so the claim is factually incorrect.

title: Owner Can Arbitrarily Change Pool Prices
reason: This is an explicit owner-only administrative power gated by 6000 blocks of inactivity, so it is a trust/governance concern rather than an unintended vulnerability.

title: Missing Access Control on Price Adjuster Role
reason: The owner granting a privileged role is expected governance behavior; no missing access control is shown in the code.

title: Top LP Removal Restriction Can Be Bypassed
reason: The proposed status-change path is owner-controlled and not the root bug. The reportable issue here is the stronger `msg.sender`/`to` mismatch already captured in F-003.

title: Insufficient Pool Size Validation
reason: The `initialPoolValue <= poolValue || poolValue >= poolSizeMinLimit` check allows trades that increase pool value even if the pool remains below the minimum, which is consistent with preventing further shrinkage rather than proving a harmful bypass.

title: Token Insurance Not Backed by Actual Collateral
reason: `tokenInsurance` is an owner-set risk limit, not a collateral vault; the report does not show a concrete exploit beyond governance misrepresentation.

title: Owner Can Pause Pools Without Time Constraint for Some Transitions
reason: This is an owner-controlled operational choice and does not itself create realistic protocol-level harm.

title: Rounding Loss in Liquidity Calculations
reason: This is ordinary integer truncation with de minimis effect and not a realistic reportable vulnerability.

title: Uninitialized feeTo Allows Immediate Loss of Fees
reason: `feeTo` being unset is a deployment/configuration issue, not an attacker-triggerable bug; moreover swap fee minting is currently disabled via `_mintFee`.
